label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to solve this problem. Let me read it again. Jane was born on the last day of February in 2000. Today is her 16-year-old birthday. What is the date one week from today in MM/DD/YYYY? Hmm. So Jane was born on February 29th, 2000, since that's the last day of February in a leap year. But today is her 16th birthday, so we need to figure out what date that would be on. Wait, but February 29th only comes every four years. So if Jane was born on February 29th, 2000, then her birthdays would fall on 2004, 2008, 2012, 2016, etc. So her 16th birthday would be in 2016 because 16 years later from 2000 is 2016. But wait, 2016 is a leap year, right? Because 2016 divided by 4 is 504, no remainder. So yes, 2016 is a leap year, so February 29th exists there. So today's date is February 29th, 2016. The question is asking for the date one week from today. Let's add a week to February 29th, 2016. Let's count the days. Adding 7 days to February 29th would take us into March. Since February 29th is the last day of February in a leap year, the next day is March 1st. So adding 7 days would be March 7th, 2016. Let me check that again. February 29th is day 0. Then March 1st is +1, 2nd is +2, 3rd is +3, 4th +4, 5th +5, 6th +6, 7th +7. So yes, one week later would be March 7th, 2016. Looking at the options, option A is 03/07/2016. But wait, let me make sure there isn't a trick here. Sometimes people might consider that if someone is born on a leap day, their birthday is considered to be either February 28th or March 1st in non-leap years. But in this case, the problem states that today is her 16th birthday, which implies that today is the actual date of her birthday. Since 2016 is a leap year, her birthday is on February 29th. Therefore, adding a week would result in March 7th, 2016. So option A would be correct. Let me check the other options to see if there's a possibility. Option D
